title: Rate counters over adequate windows
description: Apply rate()/increase() to counters across a window covering several scrape intervals.
body: Use when turning a monotonically increasing counter into a per-second rate. Do not use a rate window near or below the scrape interval — with too few samples rate() returns gaps or noisy spikes, and applying it to a gauge is meaningless. Actions: choose a window covering at least four scrape intervals, use rate() for per-second and increase() for totals, and confirm the metric is a counter. Validation signals include a stable rate across the window and no gaps from sparse samples. Risk boundary: a rate that silently returns empty makes a dashboard or alert blind — verify the window against the scrape interval before relying on it.
